aliases: Iced Milo with Extra Milo Powder

english: Iced Milo with Extra Milo Powder

food_subgroup: Hot and iced malt chocolate beverages

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Milo Dinosaur; closest_parent is the chocolate-flavoured malt beverage class (FOODON_03301638).
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKW (Cocoa and chocolate-based beverages) is the closest defensible parent; no specific Milo Dinosaur entry exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226465004 (Chocolate drink) is the closest parent concept; Milo Dinosaur as a specific preparation is not individually coded.

main_ingredients: hot or iced water or milk

serving.description: 1 cup / glass (approximately 300 ml prepared drink with extra Milo powder topping)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of Milo Dinosaur is approximately 330 g (one large cup of about 300 ml prepared iced Milo drink topped with a generous heap of undissolved Milo powder, typically 2–3 heaped teaspoons extra).

nutrient_source.title: Milo Chocolate Malt Beverage Powder — Nestle Product Nutrition Panel and USDA FoodData Central: Cocoa-malt beverage powder (SR Legacy ID 14191) combined with prepared beverage estimation
nutrient_source.organization: Nestle Singapore (product label) cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Milo Dinosaur is a prepared beverage made from Nestle Milo powder dissolved in water or milk with extra undissolved powder on top. Nutrient values are estimated for the prepared drink at approximately 10 g Milo powder per 100 ml of prepared beverage (reflecting the extra-thick Dinosaur preparation), cross-referenced against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for cocoa-malt beverage powder (NDB 14191) and Nestle Milo Singapore product nutrition panel (per 100 g powder: energy 1615 kJ / 386 kcal, protein 8.9 g, fat 6.1 g, carbohydrate 73.5 g, sugar 46.0 g, dietary fibre 1.5 g, sodium 210 mg, calcium 900 mg). Values below are for the prepared drink per 100 g (approximately 10 g powder in 90 g water/milk), adjusted for dilution. Condensed milk addition is not included in base calculation.

gi_source.title: Milo (Nestle), prepared as directed — GI value from University of Sydney GI Database; analogous estimate applied to Milo Dinosaur preparation
gi_source.source_match_notes: The University of Sydney GI Database reports a GI of approximately 55 (low) for Nestle Milo prepared as directed with water. Milo Dinosaur uses the same Milo powder base with extra undissolved powder on top, which increases carbohydrate density per serving but does not materially alter the GI of the base carbohydrate. GI of 55 is applied as the closest defensible analogous estimate. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (55 × 27.1) / 100 ≈ 14.9, where carbohydrate per 330 g serving ≈ 27.1 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium content; low per serving
hypertension.guidance: A single serving of Milo Dinosaur contributes approximately 79 mg sodium, which is low relative to the 2000 mg daily limit recommended for hypertensive individuals, but frequent consumption with added condensed milk or sweetened milk can increase sodium and caloric load and should be monitored.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content and low-to-medium glycemic index with significant glycemic load per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: Milo Dinosaur has a low GI of approximately 55 and a glycemic load of approximately 14.9 per serving; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit consumption due to the substantial sugar load (approximately 17 g sugar per serving) and the additional carbohydrate from the undissolved Milo powder topping, which can cause postprandial blood glucose elevation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat and added sugar from malt chocolate powder and optional condensed milk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Milo Dinosaur contains saturated fat from the malt chocolate powder and, when prepared with condensed or evaporated milk, delivers additional saturated fat and sugar; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit frequency of consumption and avoid the condensed milk variant to reduce saturated fat and total caloric intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Milo Dinosaur is a popular Southeast Asian iced malt chocolate beverage made from Nestle Milo powder, a malted barley and cocoa-based drink mix fortified with vitamins and minerals. The defining characteristic of the Milo Dinosaur, as opposed to a standard Iced Milo, is the generous heap of undissolved Milo powder piled on top of the prepared drink, creating a thick, grainy, intensely chocolatey layer that the drinker stirs in or eats directly. The drink originated in Singapore and Malaysia and has become widely popular across Southeast Asia including Indonesia, where it is served in kopitiams (traditional coffee shops), hawker centres, and casual restaurants. The base drink is typically prepared by dissolving Milo powder in hot water or hot milk, then pouring over ice, and finishing with a large spoonful or two of dry Milo powder on top. Some preparations add condensed milk or evaporated milk for extra sweetness and creaminess. The flavour profile is intensely malty, sweet, and chocolatey with a slightly gritty texture from the undissolved powder. Nutritionally, Milo Dinosaur is a moderate-energy beverage with meaningful carbohydrate and sugar content, making it a concern for individuals managing blood glucose. It provides calcium, B vitamins, and vitamin D from the fortified Milo powder. The glycemic index is approximately 55 (low), and a standard serving delivers a glycemic load of approximately 14.9. It is not suitable for frequent consumption by individuals with Type 2 Diabetes or those on calorie-restricted diets. The drink is halal-certified and vegetarian-friendly.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Milo Dinosaur?
What is the difference between Milo Dinosaur and Iced Milo?
What is the GI of Milo Dinosaur?
How much sugar is in Milo Dinosaur?

confidence_reason: Milo Dinosaur is a preparation-specific beverage variant not individually listed in any primary nutritional database. Nutrient values are estimated by adapting the Nestle Milo Singapore product nutrition panel (per 100 g powder) to a prepared drink dilution of approximately 10 g powder per 100 ml beverage, cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy cocoa-malt beverage data. The GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for standard Milo prepared as directed. Confidence is medium because the source match is adapted/estimated rather than exact.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are per 100 g of prepared Milo Dinosaur drink, estimated at approximately 10 g Milo powder dissolved in 90 g water/milk, reflecting the extra-thick Dinosaur preparation style. Condensed milk or evaporated milk additions are not included in the base calculation and would increase energy, sugar, fat, and sodium values. 2. trans_fat_g is 0.0; confirmed as negligible/trace per Nestle Milo Singapore product label. 3. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the Nestle Milo Singapore product nutrition panel or the USDA SR Legacy cocoa-malt beverage entry. 4. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for standard Milo prepared as directed; the Milo Dinosaur preparation increases carbohydrate density per serving but does not materially alter the GI of the base carbohydrate matrix. 5. The input `source` (field survey data) and `veritas_notes` indicate a strong Singaporean context for this record. Therefore, despite the primary `cuisine` being 'Indonesian', the mandatory multilingual requirements for Singaporean dishes (English, Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) have been applied to ensure comprehensive coverage. 6. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g values are estimated from the fat composition of cocoa-malt powder per USDA SR Legacy data, scaled to prepared drink concentration.
